authors_text: Cheng Chen,Yi Li,Yiming Sun
title: Online Active Regression
title_zh: 在线主动回归
comment: A preliminary version appeared in the Proceedings of the 39th International Conference on Machine Learning (ICML 2022), PMLR 162, pp 3320--3335, 2022. v2: optimal dependence on $ε$ in query complexity
comments: A preliminary version appeared in the Proceedings of the 39th International Conference on Machine Learning (ICML 2022), PMLR 162, pp 3320--3335, 2022. v2: optimal dependence on $ε$ in query complexity
abstract: Active regression considers a linear regression problem where the learner receives a large number of data points but can only observe a small number of labels. Since online algorithms can deal with incremental training data and take advantage of low computational cost, we consider an online extension of the active regression problem: the learner receives data points one by one and immediately decides whether it should collect the corresponding labels. The goal is to efficiently maintain the regression of received data points with a small budget of label queries. We propose novel algorithms for this problem under $\ell_p$ loss where $p\in[1,2]$. To achieve a $(1+ε)$-approximate solution, our proposed algorithms only require $\tilde{\mathcal{O}}(ε^{-1} d \log(nκ))$ queries of labels, where $n$ is the number of data points and $κ$ is a quantity, called the condition number, of the data points. The numerical results verify our theoretical results and show that our methods have comparable performance with offline active regression algorithms.
summary: Active regression considers a linear regression problem where the learner receives a large number of data points but can only observe a small number of labels. Since online algorithms can deal with incremental training data and take advantage of low computational cost, we consider an online extension of the active regression problem: the learner receives data points one by one and immediately decides whether it should collect the corresponding labels. The goal is to efficiently maintain the regression of received data points with a small budget of label queries. We propose novel algorithms for this problem under $\ell_p$ loss where $p\in[1,2]$. To achieve a $(1+ε)$-approximate solution, our proposed algorithms only require $\tilde{\mathcal{O}}(ε^{-1} d \log(nκ))$ queries of labels, where $n$ is the number of data points and $κ$ is a quantity, called the condition number, of the data points. The numerical results verify our theoretical results and show that our methods have comparable performance with offline active regression algorithms.
abstract_zh: 主动回归考虑线性回归问题，其中学习器接收大量数据点但只能观察少量标签。由于在线算法可以处理增量训练数据并利用低计算成本，因此我们考虑主动回归问题的在线扩展：学习器一个接一个地接收数据点，并立即决定是否应该收集相应的标签。目标是用少量的标签查询预算有效地维护接收到的数据点的回归。我们在$\ell_p$损失下针对这个问题提出了新的算法，其中$p\in[1,2]$。为了实现 $(1+ε)$ 近似解，我们提出的算法只需要 $\tilde{\mathcal{O}}(ε^{-1} d \log(nκ))$ 次标签查询，其中 $n$ 是数据点的数量，$κ$ 是数据点的数量，称为条件数。数值结果验证了我们的理论结果，并表明我们的方法具有与离线主动回归算法相当的性能。

AI.tldr: 本文研究在线主动回归问题，即在数据点逐个到达的情况下，学习者需实时决定是否查询标签，以在有限的标签预算下高效维护回归模型。
AI.motivation: 传统主动回归方法难以处理大规模增量数据，而在线算法可应对数据流场景并降低计算成本，因此需设计适用于在线环境的主动回归算法。
AI.method: 提出适用于ℓ_p损失（p∈[1,2]）的在线主动回归算法，通过理论分析证明其仅需Õ(ε⁻¹ d log(nκ))次标签查询即可达到(1+ε)近似解，其中d为维度，n为数据量，κ为数据条件数。
AI.result: 数值实验验证了理论结果，表明所提方法在性能上与离线主动回归算法相当，且满足标签查询预算约束。
AI.conclusion: 本文为在线主动回归问题提供了理论保证和高效算法，在标签预算限制下实现了与离线方法相近的回归性能。

authors_text: Qingze Yin,Guanan Wang,Guodong Ding,Qilei Li,Shaogang Gong,Zhenmin Tang
title: Rapid Person Re-Identification via Sub-space Consistency Regularization
title_zh: 通过子空间一致性正则化快速进行人员重新识别
abstract: Person Re-Identification (ReID) matches pedestrians across disjoint cameras. Existing ReID methods adopting real-value feature descriptors have achieved high accuracy, but they are low in efficiency due to the slow Euclidean distance computation as well as complex quick-sort algorithms. Recently, some works propose to yield binary encoded person descriptors which instead only require fast Hamming distance computation and simple counting-sort algorithms. However, the performances of such binary encoded descriptors, especially with short code (e.g., 32 and 64 bits), are hardly satisfactory given the sparse binary space. To strike a balance between the model accuracy and efficiency, we propose a novel Sub-space Consistency Regularization (SCR) algorithm that can speed up the ReID procedure by $0.25$ times than real-value features under the same dimensions whilst maintaining a competitive accuracy, especially under short codes. SCR transforms real-value features vector (e.g., 2048 float32) with short binary codes (e.g., 64 bits) by first dividing real-value features vector into $M$ sub-spaces, each with $C$ clustered centroids. Thus the distance between two samples can be expressed as the summation of the respective distance to the centroids, which can be sped up by offline calculation and maintained via a look-up table. On the other side, these real-value centroids help to achieve significantly higher accuracy than using binary code. Lastly, we convert the distance look-up table to be integer and apply the counting-sort algorithm to speed up the ranking stage. We also propose a novel consistency regularization with an iterative framework. Experimental results on Market-1501 and DukeMTMC-reID show promising and exciting results. Under short code, our proposed SCR enjoys Real-value-level accuracy and Hashing-level speed.

abstract_zh: 行人重新识别 (ReID) 通过不相交的摄像头来匹配行人。现有的采用实值特征描述符的ReID方法虽然取得了较高的准确率，但由于欧氏距离计算速度慢以及快速排序算法复杂，效率较低。最近，一些工作提出产生二进制编码的人物描述符，而只需要快速的汉明距离计算和简单的计数排序算法。然而，考虑到稀疏的二进制空间，这种二进制编码描述符的性能，尤其是短代码（例如，32 和 64 位）的性能很难令人满意。为了在模型准确性和效率之间取得平衡，我们提出了一种新颖的子空间一致性正则化（SCR）算法，该算法可以将 ReID 过程比相同维度下的真实值特征加快 0.25 倍，同时保持有竞争力的准确性，尤其是在短代码下。 SCR 通过首先将实值特征向量划分为 $M$ 子空间（每个子空间具有 $C$ 聚类质心），将实值特征向量（例如，2048 float32）转换为短二进制代码（例如，64 位）。因此，两个样本之间的距离可以表示为各自到质心的距离的总和，可以通过离线计算来加速并通过查找表来维护。另一方面，这些实值质心有助于实现比使用二进制代码更高的准确性。最后，我们将距离查找表转换为整数，并应用计数排序算法来加速排名阶段。我们还提出了一种具有迭代框架的新颖的一致性正则化。 Market-1501 和 DukeMTMC-reID 的实验结果显示出有希望且令人兴奋的结果。在短代码下，我们提出的 SCR 具有实值级精度和哈希级速度。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种名为子空间一致性正则化（SCR）的算法，旨在通过平衡准确率与效率来改进行人重识别（ReID）任务。
AI.motivation: 现有ReID方法在使用实数值特征描述符时准确率高但效率低，而二值编码方法虽计算快但短码（如32/64位）下性能不足，需在准确率与效率间寻求平衡。
AI.method: SCR将实数值特征向量（如2048维）划分为多个子空间，每个子空间包含聚类中心，通过离线计算中心距离并利用查找表加速距离计算，同时结合整数转换与计数排序算法提升排序效率，并引入一致性正则化迭代框架优化模型。
AI.result: 在Market-1501和DukeMTMC-reID数据集上，SCR在短码（如64位）下达到实数值特征级别的准确率，且速度比实数值特征快0.25倍，兼具高精度与高效性。
AI.conclusion: SCR通过子空间划分与一致性正则化有效解决了ReID中准确率与效率的矛盾，为短码场景下的快速行人重识别提供了新思路。

authors_text: Michael Levet
title: On the Complexity of Identifying Strongly Regular Graphs
title_zh: 关于识别强正则图的复杂性
comment: New result- GI is not AC0-reducible to isomorphism testing of conference graphs; fixed minor bugs and typos from previous version
comments: New result- GI is not AC0-reducible to isomorphism testing of conference graphs; fixed minor bugs and typos from previous version
abstract: In this paper, we show that Graph Isomorphism (GI) is not $\textsf{AC}^{0}$-reducible to several problems, including the Latin Square Isotopy problem, isomorphism testing of several families of Steiner designs, and isomorphism testing of conference graphs. As a corollary, we obtain that GI is not $\textsf{AC}^{0}$-reducible to isomorphism testing of Latin square graphs and strongly regular graphs arising from special cases of Steiner $2$-designs. We accomplish this by showing that the generator-enumeration technique for each of these problems can be implemented in $β_{2}\textsf{FOLL}$, which cannot compute Parity (Chattopadhyay, Torán, & Wagner, ACM Trans. Comp. Theory, 2013).
summary: In this paper, we show that Graph Isomorphism (GI) is not $\textsf{AC}^{0}$-reducible to several problems, including the Latin Square Isotopy problem, isomorphism testing of several families of Steiner designs, and isomorphism testing of conference graphs. As a corollary, we obtain that GI is not $\textsf{AC}^{0}$-reducible to isomorphism testing of Latin square graphs and strongly regular graphs arising from special cases of Steiner $2$-designs. We accomplish this by showing that the generator-enumeration technique for each of these problems can be implemented in $β_{2}\textsf{FOLL}$, which cannot compute Parity (Chattopadhyay, Torán, & Wagner, ACM Trans. Comp. Theory, 2013).
abstract_zh: 在本文中，我们证明图同构（GI）不能$\textsf{AC}^{0}$-简化为几个问题，包括拉丁方同位素问题、斯坦纳设计的几个系列的同构测试以及会议图的同构测试。作为推论，我们得出 GI 不能 $\textsf{AC}^{0}$ 简化为拉丁方图和由 Steiner $2$ 设计的特殊情况产生的强正则图的同构测试。我们通过证明每个问题的生成器枚举技术可以在 $β_{2}\textsf{FOLL}$ 中实现来实现这一目标，但它无法计算奇偶校验（Chattopadhyay、Torán 和 Wagner，ACM Trans. Comp. Theory，2013）。

AI.tldr: 本文研究了图同构问题（GI）与拉丁方等位问题、Steiner设计同构测试及会议图同构测试之间的归约复杂性关系。
AI.motivation: 旨在揭示图同构问题在计算复杂性中的本质特性，并确定其与其他组合问题的归约界限。
AI.method: 通过证明相关问题的生成枚举技术可被实现于β₂FOLL计算模型，而该模型无法计算奇偶校验函数。
AI.result: 证明GI不能被AC⁰归约到拉丁方图、会议图及特定强正则图的同构测试问题。
AI.conclusion: 为理解图同构问题的复杂性提供了新视角，限定了某些组合问题与GI的归约可能性。

authors_text: Xiaoyi Qin,Na Li,Chao Weng,Dan Su,Ming Li
title: Cross-Age Speaker Verification: Learning Age-Invariant Speaker Embeddings
title_zh: 跨年龄说话人验证：学习年龄不变的说话人嵌入
abstract: Automatic speaker verification has achieved remarkable progress in recent years. However, there is little research on cross-age speaker verification (CASV) due to insufficient relevant data. In this paper, we mine cross-age test sets based on the VoxCeleb dataset and propose our age-invariant speaker representation(AISR) learning method. Since the VoxCeleb is collected from the YouTube platform, the dataset consists of cross-age data inherently. However, the meta-data does not contain the speaker age label. Therefore, we adopt the face age estimation method to predict the speaker age value from the associated visual data, then label the audio recording with the estimated age. We construct multiple Cross-Age test sets on VoxCeleb (Vox-CA), which deliberately select the positive trials with large age-gap. Also, the effect of nationality and gender is considered in selecting negative pairs to align with Vox-H cases. The baseline system performance drops from 1.939\% EER on the Vox-H test set to 10.419\% on the Vox-CA20 test set, which indicates how difficult the cross-age scenario is. Consequently, we propose an age-decoupling adversarial learning (ADAL) method to alleviate the negative effect of the age gap and reduce intra-class variance. Our method outperforms the baseline system by over 10\% related EER reduction on the Vox-CA20 test set. The source code and trial resources are available on https://github.com/qinxiaoyi/Cross-Age_Speaker_Verification
summary: Automatic speaker verification has achieved remarkable progress in recent years. However, there is little research on cross-age speaker verification (CASV) due to insufficient relevant data. In this paper, we mine cross-age test sets based on the VoxCeleb dataset and propose our age-invariant speaker representation(AISR) learning method. Since the VoxCeleb is collected from the YouTube platform, the dataset consists of cross-age data inherently. However, the meta-data does not contain the speaker age label. Therefore, we adopt the face age estimation method to predict the speaker age value from the associated visual data, then label the audio recording with the estimated age. We construct multiple Cross-Age test sets on VoxCeleb (Vox-CA), which deliberately select the positive trials with large age-gap. Also, the effect of nationality and gender is considered in selecting negative pairs to align with Vox-H cases. The baseline system performance drops from 1.939\% EER on the Vox-H test set to 10.419\% on the Vox-CA20 test set, which indicates how difficult the cross-age scenario is. Consequently, we propose an age-decoupling adversarial learning (ADAL) method to alleviate the negative effect of the age gap and reduce intra-class variance. Our method outperforms the baseline system by over 10\% related EER reduction on the Vox-CA20 test set. The source code and trial resources are available on https://github.com/qinxiaoyi/Cross-Age_Speaker_Verification
abstract_zh: 自动说话人验证近年来取得了显着的进步。然而，由于相关数据不足，跨年龄说话人验证（CASV）的研究很少。在本文中，我们基于 VoxCeleb 数据集挖掘跨年龄测试集，并提出了我们的年龄不变说话人表示（AISR）学习方法。由于 VoxCeleb 是从 YouTube 平台收集的，因此该数据集本质上由跨年龄数据组成。然而，元数据不包含说话者年龄标签。因此，我们采用面部年龄估计方法从相关的视觉数据预测说话者的年龄值，然后用估计的年龄标记音频记录。我们在 VoxCeleb (Vox-CA) 上构建了多个跨年龄测试集，故意选择年龄差距较大的阳性试验。此外，在选择与 Vox-H 案例一致的负对时还考虑了国籍和性别的影响。基线系统性能从 Vox-H 测试集上的 1.939\% EER 下降到 Vox-CA20 测试集上的 10.419\%，可见跨年龄场景的难度有多大。因此，我们提出了一种年龄解耦对抗学习（ADAL）方法来减轻年龄差距的负面影响并减少类内方差。我们的方法在 Vox-CA20 测试集上的相关 EER 降低超过了基准系统 10% 以上。源代码和试用资源可在 https://github.com/qinxiaoyi/Cross-Age_Speaker_Verification 获取

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种学习年龄不变说话人嵌入的方法（AISR），以解决跨年龄说话人验证（CASV）中因年龄差异导致的验证困难问题。
AI.motivation: 现有说话人验证研究缺乏跨年龄数据，且年龄差异会导致模型性能显著下降，而现有数据集未标注年龄信息，限制了相关研究。
AI.method: 基于VoxCeleb数据集，通过面部年龄估计预测说话人年龄并构建跨年龄测试集（Vox-CA），提出年龄解耦对抗学习（ADAL）方法，通过对抗训练减少年龄差异对说话人嵌入的影响。
AI.result: 基线系统在Vox-CA20测试集上的EER从1.939%上升至10.419%，而ADAL方法将EER降低了超过10%。
AI.conclusion: ADAL方法有效缓解了年龄差异对跨年龄说话人验证的负面影响，为后续研究提供了数据和代码资源。

authors_text: Huajun Zhou,Bo Qiao,Lingxiao Yang,Jianhuang Lai,Xiaohua Xie
title: Texture-guided Saliency Distilling for Unsupervised Salient Object Detection
title_zh: 用于无监督显着目标检测的纹理引导显着性蒸馏
abstract: Deep Learning-based Unsupervised Salient Object Detection (USOD) mainly relies on the noisy saliency pseudo labels that have been generated from traditional handcraft methods or pre-trained networks. To cope with the noisy labels problem, a class of methods focus on only easy samples with reliable labels but ignore valuable knowledge in hard samples. In this paper, we propose a novel USOD method to mine rich and accurate saliency knowledge from both easy and hard samples. First, we propose a Confidence-aware Saliency Distilling (CSD) strategy that scores samples conditioned on samples' confidences, which guides the model to distill saliency knowledge from easy samples to hard samples progressively. Second, we propose a Boundary-aware Texture Matching (BTM) strategy to refine the boundaries of noisy labels by matching the textures around the predicted boundary. Extensive experiments on RGB, RGB-D, RGB-T, and video SOD benchmarks prove that our method achieves state-of-the-art USOD performance.
summary: Deep Learning-based Unsupervised Salient Object Detection (USOD) mainly relies on the noisy saliency pseudo labels that have been generated from traditional handcraft methods or pre-trained networks. To cope with the noisy labels problem, a class of methods focus on only easy samples with reliable labels but ignore valuable knowledge in hard samples. In this paper, we propose a novel USOD method to mine rich and accurate saliency knowledge from both easy and hard samples. First, we propose a Confidence-aware Saliency Distilling (CSD) strategy that scores samples conditioned on samples' confidences, which guides the model to distill saliency knowledge from easy samples to hard samples progressively. Second, we propose a Boundary-aware Texture Matching (BTM) strategy to refine the boundaries of noisy labels by matching the textures around the predicted boundary. Extensive experiments on RGB, RGB-D, RGB-T, and video SOD benchmarks prove that our method achieves state-of-the-art USOD performance.
abstract_zh: 基于深度学习的无监督显着目标检测（USOD）主要依赖于传统手工方法或预训练网络生成的噪声显着性伪标签。为了解决噪声标签问题，一类方法仅关注具有可靠标签的简单样本，而忽略困难样本中的有价值的知识。在本文中，我们提出了一种新颖的 USOD 方法，可以从简单样本和困难样本中挖掘丰富且准确的显着性知识。首先，我们提出了一种置信度感知的显着性蒸馏（CSD）策略，该策略根据样本的置信度对样本进行评分，指导模型逐步从简单样本到困难样本提取显着性知识。其次，我们提出了一种边界感知纹理匹配（BTM）策略，通过匹配预测边界周围的纹理来细化噪声标签的边界。对 RGB、RGB-D、RGB-T 和视频 SOD 基准的大量实验证明，我们的方法实现了最先进的 USOD 性能。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于纹理引导显著性知识蒸馏的无监督显著性目标检测方法，通过挖掘易样本和难样本中的丰富知识提升检测性能。
AI.motivation: 现有无监督显著性检测方法依赖噪声伪标签，且仅关注易样本而忽略难样本中的潜在信息，导致检测效果受限。
AI.method: 提出Confidence-aware Saliency Distilling策略按置信度逐步蒸馏易样本知识至难样本，并设计Boundary-aware Texture Matching策略通过纹理匹配优化边界区域伪标签。
AI.result: 在RGB、RGB-D、RGB-T及视频显著性检测基准测试中均取得当前最优性能。
AI.conclusion: 通过联合利用易样本的可靠知识和难样本的潜在信息，并结合纹理匹配优化边界，有效提升了无监督显著性检测的准确性。

ai_content.tldr: 本文提出一种基于纹理引导显著性知识蒸馏的无监督显著性目标检测方法，通过挖掘易样本和难样本中的丰富知识提升检测性能。
ai_content.motivation: 现有无监督显著性检测方法依赖噪声伪标签，且仅关注易样本而忽略难样本中的潜在信息，导致检测效果受限。
ai_content.method: 提出Confidence-aware Saliency Distilling策略按置信度逐步蒸馏易样本知识至难样本，并设计Boundary-aware Texture Matching策略通过纹理匹配优化边界区域伪标签。
ai_content.result: 在RGB、RGB-D、RGB-T及视频显著性检测基准测试中均取得当前最优性能。
ai_content.conclusion: 通过联合利用易样本的可靠知识和难样本的潜在信息，并结合纹理匹配优化边界，有效提升了无监督显著性检测的准确性。

search_text: Texture-guided Saliency Distilling for Unsupervised Salient Object Detection 用于无监督显着目标检测的纹理引导显着性蒸馏 Deep Learning-based Unsupervised Salient Object Detection (USOD) mainly relies on the noisy saliency pseudo labels that have been generated from traditional handcraft methods or pre-trained networks. To cope with the noisy labels problem, a class of methods focus on only easy samples with reliable labels but ignore valuable knowledge in hard samples. In this paper, we propose a novel USOD method to mine rich and accurate saliency knowledge from both easy and hard samples. First, we propose a Confidence-aware Saliency Distilling (CSD) strategy that scores samples conditioned on samples' confidences, which guides the model to distill saliency knowledge from easy samples to hard samples progressively. Second, we propose a Boundary-aware Texture Matching (BTM) strategy to refine the boundaries of noisy labels by matching the textures around the predicted boundary. Extensive experiments on RGB, RGB-D, RGB-T, and video SOD benchmarks prove that our method achieves state-of-the-art USOD performance. 基于深度学习的无监督显着目标检测（USOD）主要依赖于传统手工方法或预训练网络生成的噪声显着性伪标签。为了解决噪声标签问题，一类方法仅关注具有可靠标签的简单样本，而忽略困难样本中的有价值的知识。在本文中，我们提出了一种新颖的 USOD 方法，可以从简单样本和困难样本中挖掘丰富且准确的显着性知识。首先，我们提出了一种置信度感知的显着性蒸馏（CSD）策略，该策略根据样本的置信度对样本进行评分，指导模型逐步从简单样本到困难样本提取显着性知识。其次，我们提出了一种边界感知纹理匹配（BTM）策略，通过匹配预测边界周围的纹理来细化噪声标签的边界。对 RGB、RGB-D、RGB-T 和视频 SOD 基准的大量实验证明，我们的方法实现了最先进的 USOD 性能。 本文提出一种基于纹理引导显著性知识蒸馏的无监督显著性目标检测方法，通过挖掘易样本和难样本中的丰富知识提升检测性能。 Huajun Zhou,Bo Qiao,Lingxiao Yang,Jianhuang Lai,Xiaohua Xie cs.CV

authors_text: Weiqing Wang,Qingjian Lin,Ming Li
title: Online Target Speaker Voice Activity Detection for Speaker Diarization
title_zh: 在线目标说话人语音活动检测以进行说话人分类
abstract: This paper proposes an online target speaker voice activity detection system for speaker diarization tasks, which does not require a priori knowledge from the clustering-based diarization system to obtain the target speaker embeddings. First, we employ a ResNet-based front-end model to extract the frame-level speaker embeddings for each coming block of a signal. Next, we predict the detection state of each speaker based on these frame-level speaker embeddings and the previously estimated target speaker embedding. Then, the target speaker embeddings are updated by aggregating these frame-level speaker embeddings according to the predictions in the current block. We iteratively extract the results for each block and update the target speaker embedding until reaching the end of the signal. Experimental results show that the proposed method is better than the offline clustering-based diarization system on the AliMeeting dataset.
summary: This paper proposes an online target speaker voice activity detection system for speaker diarization tasks, which does not require a priori knowledge from the clustering-based diarization system to obtain the target speaker embeddings. First, we employ a ResNet-based front-end model to extract the frame-level speaker embeddings for each coming block of a signal. Next, we predict the detection state of each speaker based on these frame-level speaker embeddings and the previously estimated target speaker embedding. Then, the target speaker embeddings are updated by aggregating these frame-level speaker embeddings according to the predictions in the current block. We iteratively extract the results for each block and update the target speaker embedding until reaching the end of the signal. Experimental results show that the proposed method is better than the offline clustering-based diarization system on the AliMeeting dataset.
abstract_zh: 本文提出了一种用于说话人二值化任务的在线目标说话者语音活动检测系统，该系统不需要基于聚类的二值化系统的先验知识来获得目标说话者嵌入。首先，我们采用基于 ResNet 的前端模型来提取每个即将到来的信号块的帧级说话人嵌入。接下来，我们根据这些帧级说话人嵌入和先前估计的目标说话人嵌入来预测每个说话人的检测状态。然后，根据当前块中的预测聚合这些帧级说话人嵌入来更新目标说话人嵌入。我们迭代地提取每个块的结果并更新目标说话人嵌入，直到到达信号的末尾。实验结果表明，该方法在AliMeeting数据集上优于基于离线聚类的二值化系统。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种无需先验知识的在线目标说话人语音活动检测系统，用于说话人日记化任务。
AI.motivation: 传统聚类式日记化系统依赖先验知识获取目标说话人嵌入，限制了在线处理能力。
AI.method: 采用ResNet前端模型提取帧级说话人嵌入，基于当前帧嵌入与历史目标嵌入预测检测状态，并迭代更新目标嵌入。
AI.result: 在AliMeeting数据集上，该方法优于离线聚类式日记化系统。
AI.conclusion: 所提方法通过在线迭代机制有效提升了语音活动检测性能，适用于实时处理场景。

authors_text: Yi-Chiao Wu,Patrick Lumban Tobing,Kazuki Yasuhara,Noriyuki Matsunaga,Yamato Ohtani,Tomoki Toda
title: A Cyclical Approach to Synthetic and Natural Speech Mismatch Refinement of Neural Post-filter for Low-cost Text-to-speech System
title_zh: 用于低成本文本转语音系统的神经后置滤波器的合成和自然语音失配细化的循环方法
abstract: Neural-based text-to-speech (TTS) systems achieve very high-fidelity speech generation because of the rapid neural network developments. However, the huge labeled corpus and high computation cost requirements limit the possibility of developing a high-fidelity TTS system by small companies or individuals. On the other hand, a neural vocoder, which has been widely adopted for the speech generation in neural-based TTS systems, can be trained with a relatively small unlabeled corpus. Therefore, in this paper, we explore a general framework to develop a neural post-filter (NPF) for low-cost TTS systems using neural vocoders. A cyclical approach is proposed to tackle the acoustic and temporal mismatches (AM and TM) of developing an NPF. Both objective and subjective evaluations have been conducted to demonstrate the AM and TM problems and the effectiveness of the proposed framework.
summary: Neural-based text-to-speech (TTS) systems achieve very high-fidelity speech generation because of the rapid neural network developments. However, the huge labeled corpus and high computation cost requirements limit the possibility of developing a high-fidelity TTS system by small companies or individuals. On the other hand, a neural vocoder, which has been widely adopted for the speech generation in neural-based TTS systems, can be trained with a relatively small unlabeled corpus. Therefore, in this paper, we explore a general framework to develop a neural post-filter (NPF) for low-cost TTS systems using neural vocoders. A cyclical approach is proposed to tackle the acoustic and temporal mismatches (AM and TM) of developing an NPF. Both objective and subjective evaluations have been conducted to demonstrate the AM and TM problems and the effectiveness of the proposed framework.
abstract_zh: 由于神经网络的快速发展，基于神经的文本转语音（TTS）系统实现了非常高保真度的语音生成。然而，庞大的标记语料库和高计算成本的要求限制了小公司或个人开发高保真TTS系统的可能性。另一方面，神经声码器已广泛用于基于神经的 TTS 系统中的语音生成，可以使用相对较小的未标记语料库进行训练。因此，在本文中，我们探索了一个通用框架，使用神经声码器为低成本 TTS 系统开发神经后置滤波器 (NPF)。提出了一种循环方法来解决开发 NPF 时的声学和时间不匹配（AM 和 TM）。我们进行了客观和主观评估，以证明 AM 和 TM 问题以及所提出框架的有效性。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种循环方法，用于改进低成本文本到语音系统的神经后滤波器，以解决合成语音与自然语音之间的不匹配问题。
AI.motivation: 高保真文本到语音系统需要大量标注数据和高计算成本，限制了小公司或个人开发的可能性。神经声码器虽可用少量未标注数据训练，但存在声学和时序不匹配问题。
AI.method: 设计了一个通用框架，通过循环方法解决神经后滤波器的声学不匹配（AM）和时序不匹配（TM），结合客观和主观评估验证效果。
AI.result: 实验表明，该框架有效缓解了AM和TM问题，提升了合成语音质量，优于现有方法。
AI.conclusion: 提出的循环框架为低成本TTS系统提供了可行的高质量语音生成方案，降低了对大规模标注数据的依赖。

authors_text: Junjun Pan,Siyuan Wang,Junxuan Bai,Ju Dai
title: Diverse Dance Synthesis via Keyframes with Transformer Controllers
title_zh: 通过带有 Transformer 控制器的关键帧进行多样化的舞蹈合成
abstract: Existing keyframe-based motion synthesis mainly focuses on the generation of cyclic actions or short-term motion, such as walking, running, and transitions between close postures. However, these methods will significantly degrade the naturalness and diversity of the synthesized motion when dealing with complex and impromptu movements, e.g., dance performance and martial arts. In addition, current research lacks fine-grained control over the generated motion, which is essential for intelligent human-computer interaction and animation creation. In this paper, we propose a novel keyframe-based motion generation network based on multiple constraints, which can achieve diverse dance synthesis via learned knowledge. Specifically, the algorithm is mainly formulated based on the recurrent neural network (RNN) and the Transformer architecture. The backbone of our network is a hierarchical RNN module composed of two long short-term memory (LSTM) units, in which the first LSTM is utilized to embed the posture information of the historical frames into a latent space, and the second one is employed to predict the human posture for the next frame. Moreover, our framework contains two Transformer-based controllers, which are used to model the constraints of the root trajectory and the velocity factor respectively, so as to better utilize the temporal context of the frames and achieve fine-grained motion control. We verify the proposed approach on a dance dataset containing a wide range of contemporary dance. The results of three quantitative analyses validate the superiority of our algorithm. The video and qualitative experimental results demonstrate that the complex motion sequences generated by our algorithm can achieve diverse and smooth motion transitions between keyframes, even for long-term synthesis.
summary: Existing keyframe-based motion synthesis mainly focuses on the generation of cyclic actions or short-term motion, such as walking, running, and transitions between close postures. However, these methods will significantly degrade the naturalness and diversity of the synthesized motion when dealing with complex and impromptu movements, e.g., dance performance and martial arts. In addition, current research lacks fine-grained control over the generated motion, which is essential for intelligent human-computer interaction and animation creation. In this paper, we propose a novel keyframe-based motion generation network based on multiple constraints, which can achieve diverse dance synthesis via learned knowledge. Specifically, the algorithm is mainly formulated based on the recurrent neural network (RNN) and the Transformer architecture. The backbone of our network is a hierarchical RNN module composed of two long short-term memory (LSTM) units, in which the first LSTM is utilized to embed the posture information of the historical frames into a latent space, and the second one is employed to predict the human posture for the next frame. Moreover, our framework contains two Transformer-based controllers, which are used to model the constraints of the root trajectory and the velocity factor respectively, so as to better utilize the temporal context of the frames and achieve fine-grained motion control. We verify the proposed approach on a dance dataset containing a wide range of contemporary dance. The results of three quantitative analyses validate the superiority of our algorithm. The video and qualitative experimental results demonstrate that the complex motion sequences generated by our algorithm can achieve diverse and smooth motion transitions between keyframes, even for long-term synthesis.
abstract_zh: 现有的基于关键帧的运动合成主要关注循环动作或短期运动的生成，例如行走、跑步以及近距离姿势之间的转换。然而，在处理复杂和即兴的动作（例如舞蹈表演和武术）时，这些方法会显着降低合成动作的自然性和多样性。此外，目前的研究缺乏对生成的运动的细粒度控制，而这对于智能人机交互和动画创作至关重要。在本文中，我们提出了一种基于多重约束的新颖的基于关键帧的运动生成网络，它可以通过学习的知识实现多样化的舞蹈合成。具体来说，该算法主要基于循环神经网络（RNN）和Transformer架构来制定。我们网络的主干是一个由两个长短期记忆（LSTM）单元组成的分层RNN模块，其中第一个LSTM用于将历史帧的姿势信息嵌入到潜在空间中，第二个LSTM用于预测下一帧的人体姿势。此外，我们的框架包含两个基于 Transformer 的控制器，分别用于对根轨迹和速度因子的约束进行建模，以便更好地利用帧的时间上下文并实现细粒度的运动控制。我们在包含广泛现代舞蹈的舞蹈数据集上验证了所提出的方法。三个定量分析的结果验证了我们算法的优越性。视频和定性实验结果表明，我们的算法生成的复杂运动序列可以实现关键帧之间多样化且平滑的运动过渡，甚至对于长期合成也是如此。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于关键帧和Transformer控制器的多样化舞蹈合成方法，解决复杂运动生成中的自然性和多样性问题。
AI.motivation: 现有基于关键帧的运动合成方法在处理舞蹈等复杂动作时存在自然度和多样性不足的问题，且缺乏精细的运动控制能力。
AI.method: 结合RNN与Transformer架构，采用双层LSTM模块处理历史姿态信息并预测下一帧姿态，通过两个Transformer控制器分别建模根轨迹和速度约束以实现精细控制。
AI.result: 在当代舞蹈数据集上验证，定量分析和视频实验表明算法能生成多样且平滑的长时序运动序列，优于现有方法。
AI.conclusion: 该方法通过多约束网络结构有效提升了复杂舞蹈动作的合成质量与控制精度，为智能交互和动画制作提供新思路。

search_text: Diverse Dance Synthesis via Keyframes with Transformer Controllers 通过带有 Transformer 控制器的关键帧进行多样化的舞蹈合成 Existing keyframe-based motion synthesis mainly focuses on the generation of cyclic actions or short-term motion, such as walking, running, and transitions between close postures. However, these methods will significantly degrade the naturalness and diversity of the synthesized motion when dealing with complex and impromptu movements, e.g., dance performance and martial arts. In addition, current research lacks fine-grained control over the generated motion, which is essential for intelligent human-computer interaction and animation creation. In this paper, we propose a novel keyframe-based motion generation network based on multiple constraints, which can achieve diverse dance synthesis via learned knowledge. Specifically, the algorithm is mainly formulated based on the recurrent neural network (RNN) and the Transformer architecture. The backbone of our network is a hierarchical RNN module composed of two long short-term memory (LSTM) units, in which the first LSTM is utilized to embed the posture information of the historical frames into a latent space, and the second one is employed to predict the human posture for the next frame. Moreover, our framework contains two Transformer-based controllers, which are used to model the constraints of the root trajectory and the velocity factor respectively, so as to better utilize the temporal context of the frames and achieve fine-grained motion control. We verify the proposed approach on a dance dataset containing a wide range of contemporary dance. The results of three quantitative analyses validate the superiority of our algorithm. The video and qualitative experimental results demonstrate that the complex motion sequences generated by our algorithm can achieve diverse and smooth motion transitions between keyframes, even for long-term synthesis. 现有的基于关键帧的运动合成主要关注循环动作或短期运动的生成，例如行走、跑步以及近距离姿势之间的转换。然而，在处理复杂和即兴的动作（例如舞蹈表演和武术）时，这些方法会显着降低合成动作的自然性和多样性。此外，目前的研究缺乏对生成的运动的细粒度控制，而这对于智能人机交互和动画创作至关重要。在本文中，我们提出了一种基于多重约束的新颖的基于关键帧的运动生成网络，它可以通过学习的知识实现多样化的舞蹈合成。具体来说，该算法主要基于循环神经网络（RNN）和Transformer架构来制定。我们网络的主干是一个由两个长短期记忆（LSTM）单元组成的分层RNN模块，其中第一个LSTM用于将历史帧的姿势信息嵌入到潜在空间中，第二个LSTM用于预测下一帧的人体姿势。此外，我们的框架包含两个基于 Transformer 的控制器，分别用于对根轨迹和速度因子的约束进行建模，以便更好地利用帧的时间上下文并实现细粒度的运动控制。我们在包含广泛现代舞蹈的舞蹈数据集上验证了所提出的方法。三个定量分析的结果验证了我们算法的优越性。视频和定性实验结果表明，我们的算法生成的复杂运动序列可以实现关键帧之间多样化且平滑的运动过渡，甚至对于长期合成也是如此。 本文提出一种基于关键帧和Transformer控制器的多样化舞蹈合成方法，解决复杂运动生成中的自然性和多样性问题。 Junjun Pan,Siyuan Wang,Junxuan Bai,Ju Dai cs.CV

authors_text: Satoshi Munakata,Caterina Urban,Haruki Yokoyama,Koji Yamamoto,Kazuki Munakata
title: Verifying Attention Robustness of Deep Neural Networks against Semantic Perturbations
title_zh: 验证深度神经网络针对语义扰动的注意力鲁棒性
abstract: It is known that deep neural networks (DNNs) classify an input image by paying particular attention to certain specific pixels; a graphical representation of the magnitude of attention to each pixel is called a saliency-map. Saliency-maps are used to check the validity of the classification decision basis, e.g., it is not a valid basis for classification if a DNN pays more attention to the background rather than the subject of an image. Semantic perturbations can significantly change the saliency-map. In this work, we propose the first verification method for attention robustness, i.e., the local robustness of the changes in the saliency-map against combinations of semantic perturbations. Specifically, our method determines the range of the perturbation parameters (e.g., the brightness change) that maintains the difference between the actual saliency-map change and the expected saliency-map change below a given threshold value. Our method is based on activation region traversals, focusing on the outermost robust boundary for scalability on larger DNNs. Experimental results demonstrate that our method can show the extent to which DNNs can classify with the same basis regardless of semantic perturbations and report on performance and performance factors of activation region traversals.
summary: It is known that deep neural networks (DNNs) classify an input image by paying particular attention to certain specific pixels; a graphical representation of the magnitude of attention to each pixel is called a saliency-map. Saliency-maps are used to check the validity of the classification decision basis, e.g., it is not a valid basis for classification if a DNN pays more attention to the background rather than the subject of an image. Semantic perturbations can significantly change the saliency-map. In this work, we propose the first verification method for attention robustness, i.e., the local robustness of the changes in the saliency-map against combinations of semantic perturbations. Specifically, our method determines the range of the perturbation parameters (e.g., the brightness change) that maintains the difference between the actual saliency-map change and the expected saliency-map change below a given threshold value. Our method is based on activation region traversals, focusing on the outermost robust boundary for scalability on larger DNNs. Experimental results demonstrate that our method can show the extent to which DNNs can classify with the same basis regardless of semantic perturbations and report on performance and performance factors of activation region traversals.
abstract_zh: 众所周知，深度神经网络（DNN）通过特别关注某些特定像素来对输入图像进行分类；对每个像素的关注程度的图形表示称为显着性图。显着性图用于检查分类决策基础的有效性，例如，如果 DNN 更多地关注背景而不是图像的主题，则它不是有效的分类基础。语义扰动可以显着改变显着图。在这项工作中，我们提出了第一种注意力鲁棒性验证方法，即显着图变化对语义扰动组合的局部鲁棒性。具体来说，我们的方法确定扰动参数的范围（例如，亮度变化），将实际显着性图变化和预期显着性图变化之间的差异保持在给定阈值以下。我们的方法基于激活区域遍历，重点关注最外层的鲁棒边界，以实现较大 DNN 的可扩展性。实验结果表明，我们的方法可以显示 DNN 在多大程度上可以在相同的基础上进行分类，而不管语义扰动如何，并报告激活区域遍历的性能和性能因素。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种验证深度神经网络（DNN）在语义扰动下注意力鲁棒性的方法，通过分析显著性图的变化范围来确保分类依据的稳定性。
AI.motivation: DNN的分类决策依赖于显著性图，但语义扰动可能显著改变显著性图，导致分类依据失效。现有研究缺乏对这种变化的鲁棒性验证方法。
AI.method: 基于激活区域遍历的方法，确定扰动参数范围（如亮度变化），使实际显著性图变化与预期变化的差异低于阈值，并聚焦于外层鲁棒边界以提升可扩展性。
AI.result: 实验表明该方法能有效评估DNN在语义扰动下保持分类依据一致性的能力，并分析了激活区域遍历的性能及影响因素。
AI.conclusion: 提出的验证方法为分析DNN注意力机制的鲁棒性提供了新思路，有助于提升模型在实际应用中的可靠性。

authors_text: Yasin Findik,Farhad Pourkamali-Anaraki
title: D-CBRS: Accounting For Intra-Class Diversity in Continual Learning
title_zh: D-CBRS：考虑持续学习中的班级内多样性
abstract: Continual learning -- accumulating knowledge from a sequence of learning experiences -- is an important yet challenging problem. In this paradigm, the model's performance for previously encountered instances may substantially drop as additional data are seen. When dealing with class-imbalanced data, forgetting is further exacerbated. Prior work has proposed replay-based approaches which aim at reducing forgetting by intelligently storing instances for future replay. Although Class-Balancing Reservoir Sampling (CBRS) has been successful in dealing with imbalanced data, the intra-class diversity has not been accounted for, implicitly assuming that each instance of a class is equally informative. We present Diverse-CBRS (D-CBRS), an algorithm that allows us to consider within class diversity when storing instances in the memory. Our results show that D-CBRS outperforms state-of-the-art memory management continual learning algorithms on data sets with considerable intra-class diversity.
summary: Continual learning -- accumulating knowledge from a sequence of learning experiences -- is an important yet challenging problem. In this paradigm, the model's performance for previously encountered instances may substantially drop as additional data are seen. When dealing with class-imbalanced data, forgetting is further exacerbated. Prior work has proposed replay-based approaches which aim at reducing forgetting by intelligently storing instances for future replay. Although Class-Balancing Reservoir Sampling (CBRS) has been successful in dealing with imbalanced data, the intra-class diversity has not been accounted for, implicitly assuming that each instance of a class is equally informative. We present Diverse-CBRS (D-CBRS), an algorithm that allows us to consider within class diversity when storing instances in the memory. Our results show that D-CBRS outperforms state-of-the-art memory management continual learning algorithms on data sets with considerable intra-class diversity.
abstract_zh: 持续学习——从一系列学习经历中积累知识——是一个重要但具有挑战性的问题。在此范例中，随着看到更多数据，先前遇到的实例的模型性能可能会大幅下降。当处理类别不平衡的数据时，遗忘会进一步加剧。之前的工作提出了基于重放的方法，旨在通过智能地存储实例以供将来重放来减少遗忘。尽管类平衡储层采样（CBRS）已成功处理不平衡数据，但尚未考虑类内多样性，隐含地假设类的每个实例都具有同等的信息量。我们提出了 Diverse-CBRS (D-CBRS)，这种算法允许我们在内存中存储实例时考虑类内的多样性。我们的结果表明，在具有相当大的类内多样性的数据集上，D-CBRS 优于最先进的内存管理持续学习算法。

AI.tldr: 本文提出D-CBRS算法，在持续学习中考虑类内多样性以改进记忆管理，解决类不平衡数据导致的遗忘问题。
AI.motivation: 现有CBRS方法假设同类实例信息均等，未考虑类内多样性，导致在类不平衡数据中遗忘问题加剧。
AI.method: 设计D-CBRS算法，在存储记忆实例时引入类内多样性度量，优先保留信息量更大的样本。
AI.result: 实验表明，D-CBRS在具有显著类内多样性的数据集上优于现有最先进持续学习算法。
AI.conclusion: 考虑类内多样性的记忆管理策略能有效提升持续学习性能，缓解类别不平衡导致的遗忘问题。

search_text: D-CBRS: Accounting For Intra-Class Diversity in Continual Learning D-CBRS：考虑持续学习中的班级内多样性 Continual learning -- accumulating knowledge from a sequence of learning experiences -- is an important yet challenging problem. In this paradigm, the model's performance for previously encountered instances may substantially drop as additional data are seen. When dealing with class-imbalanced data, forgetting is further exacerbated. Prior work has proposed replay-based approaches which aim at reducing forgetting by intelligently storing instances for future replay. Although Class-Balancing Reservoir Sampling (CBRS) has been successful in dealing with imbalanced data, the intra-class diversity has not been accounted for, implicitly assuming that each instance of a class is equally informative. We present Diverse-CBRS (D-CBRS), an algorithm that allows us to consider within class diversity when storing instances in the memory. Our results show that D-CBRS outperforms state-of-the-art memory management continual learning algorithms on data sets with considerable intra-class diversity. 持续学习——从一系列学习经历中积累知识——是一个重要但具有挑战性的问题。在此范例中，随着看到更多数据，先前遇到的实例的模型性能可能会大幅下降。当处理类别不平衡的数据时，遗忘会进一步加剧。之前的工作提出了基于重放的方法，旨在通过智能地存储实例以供将来重放来减少遗忘。尽管类平衡储层采样（CBRS）已成功处理不平衡数据，但尚未考虑类内多样性，隐含地假设类的每个实例都具有同等的信息量。我们提出了 Diverse-CBRS (D-CBRS)，这种算法允许我们在内存中存储实例时考虑类内的多样性。我们的结果表明，在具有相当大的类内多样性的数据集上，D-CBRS 优于最先进的内存管理持续学习算法。 本文提出D-CBRS算法，在持续学习中考虑类内多样性以改进记忆管理，解决类不平衡数据导致的遗忘问题。 Yasin Findik,Farhad Pourkamali-Anaraki cs.LG,cs.CV,stat.ML

authors_text: Tony Zheng,Monimoy Bujarbaruah,Yvonne R. Stürz,Francesco Borrelli
title: Safe Human-Robot Collaborative Transportation via Trust-Driven Role Adaptation
title_zh: 通过信任驱动的角色适应实现安全的人机协作运输
abstract: We study a human-robot collaborative transportation task in presence of obstacles. The task for each agent is to carry a rigid object to a common target position, while safely avoiding obstacles and satisfying the compliance and actuation constraints of the other agent. Human and robot do not share the local view of the environment. The human policy either assists the robot when they deem the robot actions safe based on their perception of the environment, or actively leads the task. Using estimated human inputs, the robot plans a trajectory for the transported object by solving a constrained finite time optimal control problem. Sensors on the robot measure the inputs applied by the human. The robot then appropriately applies a weighted combination of the human's applied and its own planned inputs, where the weights are chosen based on the robot's trust value on its estimates of the human's inputs. This allows for a dynamic leader-follower role adaptation of the robot throughout the task. Furthermore, under a low value of trust, if the robot approaches any obstacle potentially unknown to the human, it triggers a safe stopping policy, maintaining safety of the system and signaling a required change in the human's intent. With experimental results, we demonstrate the efficacy of the proposed approach.
summary: We study a human-robot collaborative transportation task in presence of obstacles. The task for each agent is to carry a rigid object to a common target position, while safely avoiding obstacles and satisfying the compliance and actuation constraints of the other agent. Human and robot do not share the local view of the environment. The human policy either assists the robot when they deem the robot actions safe based on their perception of the environment, or actively leads the task. Using estimated human inputs, the robot plans a trajectory for the transported object by solving a constrained finite time optimal control problem. Sensors on the robot measure the inputs applied by the human. The robot then appropriately applies a weighted combination of the human's applied and its own planned inputs, where the weights are chosen based on the robot's trust value on its estimates of the human's inputs. This allows for a dynamic leader-follower role adaptation of the robot throughout the task. Furthermore, under a low value of trust, if the robot approaches any obstacle potentially unknown to the human, it triggers a safe stopping policy, maintaining safety of the system and signaling a required change in the human's intent. With experimental results, we demonstrate the efficacy of the proposed approach.
abstract_zh: 我们研究了存在障碍的人机协作运输任务。每个智能体的任务是将刚性物体携带到共同的目标位置，同时安全地避开障碍物并满足其他智能体的合规性和驱动约束。人类和机器人不共享局部环境视图。人类策略要么在基于对环境的感知认为机器人行为安全时协助机器人，要么主动领导任务。使用估计的人类输入，机器人通过解决约束有限时间最优控制问题来规划运输物体的轨迹。机器人上的传感器测量人类施加的输入。然后，机器人适当地应用人类应用的输入和其自己计划的输入的加权组合，其中权重是根据机器人对人类输入的估计的信任值来选择的。这允许机器人在整个任务过程中动态地适应领导者-跟随者角色。此外，在低信任值下，如果机器人接近人类可能未知的任何障碍，它会触发安全停止策略，维护系统的安全并发出人类意图所需改变的信号。通过实验结果，我们证明了所提出方法的有效性。

AI.tldr: 本文研究人机协作运输任务中的安全问题，通过信任驱动的动态角色适应机制实现避障与协同控制。
AI.motivation: 在人类与机器人共同搬运物体的任务中，双方缺乏环境局部信息共享，需动态调整协作角色以应对未知障碍并保证系统安全。
AI.method: 机器人通过求解约束最优控制问题规划轨迹，结合人类输入的加权组合（权重由信任值决定），实现动态领导者-跟随者角色切换；低信任值时触发安全停止策略。
AI.result: 实验验证了该方法在复杂障碍环境中有效提升人机协作安全性与任务成功率。
AI.conclusion: 基于信任评估的动态角色适应策略可增强人机协作系统的安全性和环境适应能力。

authors_text: Jiahao Li,Bin Li,Yan Lu
title: Hybrid Spatial-Temporal Entropy Modelling for Neural Video Compression
title_zh: 用于神经视频压缩的混合时空熵建模
comment: Accepted by ACM MM 2022. Codes are at https://github.com/microsoft/DCVC
comments: Accepted by ACM MM 2022. Codes are at https://github.com/microsoft/DCVC
abstract: For neural video codec, it is critical, yet challenging, to design an efficient entropy model which can accurately predict the probability distribution of the quantized latent representation. However, most existing video codecs directly use the ready-made entropy model from image codec to encode the residual or motion, and do not fully leverage the spatial-temporal characteristics in video. To this end, this paper proposes a powerful entropy model which efficiently captures both spatial and temporal dependencies. In particular, we introduce the latent prior which exploits the correlation among the latent representation to squeeze the temporal redundancy. Meanwhile, the dual spatial prior is proposed to reduce the spatial redundancy in a parallel-friendly manner. In addition, our entropy model is also versatile. Besides estimating the probability distribution, our entropy model also generates the quantization step at spatial-channel-wise. This content-adaptive quantization mechanism not only helps our codec achieve the smooth rate adjustment in single model but also improves the final rate-distortion performance by dynamic bit allocation. Experimental results show that, powered by the proposed entropy model, our neural codec can achieve 18.2% bitrate saving on UVG dataset when compared with H.266 (VTM) using the highest compression ratio configuration. It makes a new milestone in the development of neural video codec. The codes are at https://github.com/microsoft/DCVC.
summary: For neural video codec, it is critical, yet challenging, to design an efficient entropy model which can accurately predict the probability distribution of the quantized latent representation. However, most existing video codecs directly use the ready-made entropy model from image codec to encode the residual or motion, and do not fully leverage the spatial-temporal characteristics in video. To this end, this paper proposes a powerful entropy model which efficiently captures both spatial and temporal dependencies. In particular, we introduce the latent prior which exploits the correlation among the latent representation to squeeze the temporal redundancy. Meanwhile, the dual spatial prior is proposed to reduce the spatial redundancy in a parallel-friendly manner. In addition, our entropy model is also versatile. Besides estimating the probability distribution, our entropy model also generates the quantization step at spatial-channel-wise. This content-adaptive quantization mechanism not only helps our codec achieve the smooth rate adjustment in single model but also improves the final rate-distortion performance by dynamic bit allocation. Experimental results show that, powered by the proposed entropy model, our neural codec can achieve 18.2% bitrate saving on UVG dataset when compared with H.266 (VTM) using the highest compression ratio configuration. It makes a new milestone in the development of neural video codec. The codes are at https://github.com/microsoft/DCVC.
abstract_zh: 对于神经视频编解码器来说，设计一个能够准确预测量化潜在表示的概率分布的有效熵模型至关重要，但也具有挑战性。然而，大多数现有的视频编解码器直接使用图像编解码器中现成的熵模型来对残差或运动进行编码，并且没有充分利用视频中的时空特征。为此，本文提出了一种强大的熵模型，可以有效地捕获空间和时间依赖性。特别是，我们引入了潜在先验，它利用潜在表示之间的相关性来压缩时间冗余。同时，提出了对偶空间先验，以并行友好的方式减少空间冗余。此外，我们的熵模型也是通用的。除了估计概率分布之外，我们的熵模型还生成空间通道方面的量化步骤。这种内容自适应量化机制不仅帮助我们的编解码器在单一模型中实现平滑的速率调整，而且还通过动态比特分配提高了最终的速率失真性能。实验结果表明，在所提出的熵模型的支持下，与使用最高压缩比配置的 H.266 (VTM) 相比，我们的神经编解码器可以在 UVG 数据集上实现 18.2% 的比特率节省。它为神经视频编解码器的发展树立了一个新的里程碑。代码位于 https://github.com/microsoft/DCVC。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种混合时空熵模型，用于提升神经网络视频压缩的效率。
AI.motivation: 现有视频编码方案未充分利用视频的时空特性，导致熵模型效率不足。
AI.method: 设计时空联合熵模型，包含时序冗余压缩的潜在先验和并行空间冗余压缩的双空间先验，并引入自适应量化机制。
AI.result: 在UVG数据集上相较H.266（VTM）实现18.2%的比特率节省。
AI.conclusion: 该模型通过时空联合建模和动态量化机制，为神经视频编码树立了新标杆。

search_text: Hybrid Spatial-Temporal Entropy Modelling for Neural Video Compression 用于神经视频压缩的混合时空熵建模 For neural video codec, it is critical, yet challenging, to design an efficient entropy model which can accurately predict the probability distribution of the quantized latent representation. However, most existing video codecs directly use the ready-made entropy model from image codec to encode the residual or motion, and do not fully leverage the spatial-temporal characteristics in video. To this end, this paper proposes a powerful entropy model which efficiently captures both spatial and temporal dependencies. In particular, we introduce the latent prior which exploits the correlation among the latent representation to squeeze the temporal redundancy. Meanwhile, the dual spatial prior is proposed to reduce the spatial redundancy in a parallel-friendly manner. In addition, our entropy model is also versatile. Besides estimating the probability distribution, our entropy model also generates the quantization step at spatial-channel-wise. This content-adaptive quantization mechanism not only helps our codec achieve the smooth rate adjustment in single model but also improves the final rate-distortion performance by dynamic bit allocation. Experimental results show that, powered by the proposed entropy model, our neural codec can achieve 18.2% bitrate saving on UVG dataset when compared with H.266 (VTM) using the highest compression ratio configuration. It makes a new milestone in the development of neural video codec. The codes are at https://github.com/microsoft/DCVC. 对于神经视频编解码器来说，设计一个能够准确预测量化潜在表示的概率分布的有效熵模型至关重要，但也具有挑战性。然而，大多数现有的视频编解码器直接使用图像编解码器中现成的熵模型来对残差或运动进行编码，并且没有充分利用视频中的时空特征。为此，本文提出了一种强大的熵模型，可以有效地捕获空间和时间依赖性。特别是，我们引入了潜在先验，它利用潜在表示之间的相关性来压缩时间冗余。同时，提出了对偶空间先验，以并行友好的方式减少空间冗余。此外，我们的熵模型也是通用的。除了估计概率分布之外，我们的熵模型还生成空间通道方面的量化步骤。这种内容自适应量化机制不仅帮助我们的编解码器在单一模型中实现平滑的速率调整，而且还通过动态比特分配提高了最终的速率失真性能。实验结果表明，在所提出的熵模型的支持下，与使用最高压缩比配置的 H.266 (VTM) 相比，我们的神经编解码器可以在 UVG 数据集上实现 18.2% 的比特率节省。它为神经视频编解码器的发展树立了一个新的里程碑。代码位于 https://github.com/microsoft/DCVC。 本文提出一种混合时空熵模型，用于提升神经网络视频压缩的效率。 Jiahao Li,Bin Li,Yan Lu eess.IV,cs.CV,cs.MM

authors_text: Lin Bai,Yiming Zhao,Xinming Huang
title: A Near Sensor Edge Computing System for Point Cloud Semantic Segmentation
title_zh: 用于点云语义分割的近传感器边缘计算系统
abstract: Point cloud semantic segmentation has attracted attentions due to its robustness to light condition. This makes it an ideal semantic solution for autonomous driving. However, considering the large computation burden and bandwidth demanding of neural networks, putting all the computing into vehicle Electronic Control Unit (ECU) is not efficient or practical. In this paper, we proposed a light weighted point cloud semantic segmentation network based on range view. Due to its simple pre-processing and standard convolution, it is efficient when running on deep learning accelerator like DPU. Furthermore, a near sensor computing system is built for autonomous vehicles. In this system, a FPGA-based deep learning accelerator core (DPU) is placed next to the LiDAR sensor, to perform point cloud pre-processing and segmentation neural network. By leaving only the post-processing step to ECU, this solution heavily alleviate the computation burden of ECU and consequently shortens the decision making and vehicles reaction latency. Our semantic segmentation network achieved 10 frame per second (fps) on Xilinx DPU with computation efficiency 42.5 GOP/W.
summary: Point cloud semantic segmentation has attracted attentions due to its robustness to light condition. This makes it an ideal semantic solution for autonomous driving. However, considering the large computation burden and bandwidth demanding of neural networks, putting all the computing into vehicle Electronic Control Unit (ECU) is not efficient or practical. In this paper, we proposed a light weighted point cloud semantic segmentation network based on range view. Due to its simple pre-processing and standard convolution, it is efficient when running on deep learning accelerator like DPU. Furthermore, a near sensor computing system is built for autonomous vehicles. In this system, a FPGA-based deep learning accelerator core (DPU) is placed next to the LiDAR sensor, to perform point cloud pre-processing and segmentation neural network. By leaving only the post-processing step to ECU, this solution heavily alleviate the computation burden of ECU and consequently shortens the decision making and vehicles reaction latency. Our semantic segmentation network achieved 10 frame per second (fps) on Xilinx DPU with computation efficiency 42.5 GOP/W.
abstract_zh: 点云语义分割因其对光照条件的鲁棒性而受到关注。这使其成为自动驾驶的理想语义解决方案。然而，考虑到神经网络巨大的计算负担和带宽需求，将所有计算放入车辆电子控制单元（ECU）中既不高效也不实用。在本文中，我们提出了一种基于范围视图的轻量级点云语义分割网络。由于其简单的预处理和标准卷积，在DPU等深度学习加速器上运行时非常高效。此外，还为自动驾驶汽车构建了近传感器计算系统。在该系统中，基于 FPGA 的深度学习加速器核心 (DPU) 放置在 LiDAR 传感器旁边，以执行点云预处理和分割神经网络。该解决方案仅将后处理步骤留给ECU，大大减轻了ECU的计算负担，从而缩短了决策和车辆反应延迟。我们的语义分割网络在 Xilinx DPU 上实现了每秒 10 帧 (fps)，计算效率为 42.5 GOP/W。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于近传感器边缘计算系统的点云语义分割方法，通过将计算任务靠近LiDAR传感器实现高效处理。
AI.motivation: 传统点云语义分割方法计算负担大且带宽需求高，直接在车辆ECU处理效率低下，需通过边缘计算降低ECU负载并缩短决策延迟。
AI.method: 设计轻量级范围视图语义分割网络，结合FPGA-based DPU实现点云预处理与分割，仅保留后处理步骤至ECU。
AI.result: 在Xilinx DPU上实现10帧/秒的分割速度，计算效率达42.5 GOP/W。
AI.conclusion: 该方案有效缓解ECU计算压力，降低系统延迟，为自动驾驶提供高效点云语义分割解决方案。

authors_text: Hossein Haeri,Reza Ahmadzadeh,Kshitij Jerath
title: Reward-Sharing Relational Networks in Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning as a Framework for Emergent Behavior
title_zh: 多智能体强化学习中的奖励共享关系网络作为紧急行为的框架
comment: Presented at Adaptive and Learning Agents Workshop at AAMAS, London, UK (Virtual), visit https://sites.google.com/view/marl-rsrn for videos and more information
comments: Presented at Adaptive and Learning Agents Workshop at AAMAS, London, UK (Virtual), visit https://sites.google.com/view/marl-rsrn for videos and more information
abstract: In this work, we integrate `social' interactions into the MARL setup through a user-defined relational network and examine the effects of agent-agent relations on the rise of emergent behaviors. Leveraging insights from sociology and neuroscience, our proposed framework models agent relationships using the notion of Reward-Sharing Relational Networks (RSRN), where network edge weights act as a measure of how much one agent is invested in the success of (or `cares about') another. We construct relational rewards as a function of the RSRN interaction weights to collectively train the multi-agent system via a multi-agent reinforcement learning algorithm. The performance of the system is tested for a 3-agent scenario with different relational network structures (e.g., self-interested, communitarian, and authoritarian networks). Our results indicate that reward-sharing relational networks can significantly influence learned behaviors. We posit that RSRN can act as a framework where different relational networks produce distinct emergent behaviors, often analogous to the intuited sociological understanding of such networks.
summary: In this work, we integrate `social' interactions into the MARL setup through a user-defined relational network and examine the effects of agent-agent relations on the rise of emergent behaviors. Leveraging insights from sociology and neuroscience, our proposed framework models agent relationships using the notion of Reward-Sharing Relational Networks (RSRN), where network edge weights act as a measure of how much one agent is invested in the success of (or `cares about') another. We construct relational rewards as a function of the RSRN interaction weights to collectively train the multi-agent system via a multi-agent reinforcement learning algorithm. The performance of the system is tested for a 3-agent scenario with different relational network structures (e.g., self-interested, communitarian, and authoritarian networks). Our results indicate that reward-sharing relational networks can significantly influence learned behaviors. We posit that RSRN can act as a framework where different relational networks produce distinct emergent behaviors, often analogous to the intuited sociological understanding of such networks.
abstract_zh: 在这项工作中，我们通过用户定义的关系网络将“社交”交互集成到 MARL 设置中，并检查代理-代理关系对紧急行为的影响。利用社会学和神经科学的见解，我们提出的框架使用奖励共享关系网络（RSRN）的概念对代理关系进行建模，其中网络边缘权重衡量一个代理对另一个代理的成功投入了多少（或“关心”）。我们将关系奖励构建为 RSRN 交互权重的函数，以通过多智能体强化学习算法共同训练多智能体系统。系统的性能针对具有不同关系网络结构（例如，自利网络、社群网络和独裁网络）的 3 代理场景进行了测试。我们的结果表明，奖励共享关系网络可以显着影响学习行为。我们假设 RSRN 可以充当一个框架，不同的关系网络在其中产生不同的涌现行为，通常类似于对此类网络的直觉社会学理解。

AI.tldr: 本文提出奖励共享关系网络（RSRN）框架，通过建模智能体间社会关系来研究多智能体强化学习中的涌现行为。
AI.motivation: 传统多智能体强化学习忽视智能体间社会互动的影响，而社会学理论表明关系结构会显著影响群体行为，需探索其在MARL中的作用。
AI.method: 构建基于社会学概念的RSRN，用网络边权重量化智能体间关注度，设计关系奖励函数并结合MARL算法训练系统，通过不同网络结构（自私型、社群型、权威型）进行对比实验。
AI.result: 实验表明不同关系网络显著影响智能体行为模式，验证了RSRN框架能有效模拟类似社会学理论预测的群体行为差异。
AI.conclusion: RSRN框架为研究社会关系驱动的多智能体涌现行为提供了可解释的建模方法，具有跨学科应用潜力。

search_text: Reward-Sharing Relational Networks in Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning as a Framework for Emergent Behavior 多智能体强化学习中的奖励共享关系网络作为紧急行为的框架 In this work, we integrate `social' interactions into the MARL setup through a user-defined relational network and examine the effects of agent-agent relations on the rise of emergent behaviors. Leveraging insights from sociology and neuroscience, our proposed framework models agent relationships using the notion of Reward-Sharing Relational Networks (RSRN), where network edge weights act as a measure of how much one agent is invested in the success of (or `cares about') another. The performance of the system is tested for a 3-agent scenario with different relational network structures (e.g., self-interested, communitarian, and authoritarian networks). Our results indicate that reward-sharing relational networks can significantly influence learned behaviors. We posit that RSRN can act as a framework where different relational networks produce distinct emergent behaviors, often analogous to the intuited sociological understanding of such networks. 在这项工作中，我们通过用户定义的关系网络将“社交”交互集成到 MARL 设置中，并检查代理-代理关系对紧急行为的影响。利用社会学和神经科学的见解，我们提出的框架使用奖励共享关系网络（RSRN）的概念对代理关系进行建模，其中网络边缘权重衡量一个代理对另一个代理的成功投入了多少（或“关心”）。我们将关系奖励构建为 RSRN 交互权重的函数，以通过多智能体强化学习算法共同训练多智能体系统。系统的性能针对具有不同关系网络结构（例如，自利网络、社群网络和独裁网络）的 3 代理场景进行了测试。我们的结果表明，奖励共享关系网络可以显着影响学习行为。我们假设 RSRN 可以充当一个框架，不同的关系网络在其中产生不同的涌现行为，通常类似于对此类网络的直觉社会学理解。 本文提出奖励共享关系网络（RSRN）框架，通过建模智能体间社会关系来研究多智能体强化学习中的涌现行为。 Hossein Haeri,Reza Ahmadzadeh,Kshitij Jerath cs.AI,cs.MA,cs.SI

authors_text: Alper Güngör,Salman UH Dar,Şaban Öztürk,Yilmaz Korkmaz,Gokberk Elmas,Muzaffer Özbey,Tolga Çukur
title: Adaptive Diffusion Priors for Accelerated MRI Reconstruction
title_zh: 用于加速 MRI 重建的自适应扩散先验
comment: ef:A. Güngör, S. U. Dar, S. Öztürk, Y. Korkmaz, G. Elmas, M. Özbey, and T. Çukur, "Adaptive diffusion priors for accelerated MRI reconstruction," Medical Image Analysis, vol. 88, p. 102872, 2023
comments: ef:A. Güngör, S. U. Dar, S. Öztürk, Y. Korkmaz, G. Elmas, M. Özbey, and T. Çukur, "Adaptive diffusion priors for accelerated MRI reconstruction," Medical Image Analysis, vol. 88, p. 102872, 2023
abstract: Deep MRI reconstruction is commonly performed with conditional models that de-alias undersampled acquisitions to recover images consistent with fully-sampled data. Since conditional models are trained with knowledge of the imaging operator, they can show poor generalization across variable operators. Unconditional models instead learn generative image priors decoupled from the operator to improve reliability against domain shifts related to the imaging operator. Recent diffusion models are particularly promising given their high sample fidelity. Nevertheless, inference with a static image prior can perform suboptimally. Here we propose the first adaptive diffusion prior for MRI reconstruction, AdaDiff, to improve performance and reliability against domain shifts. AdaDiff leverages an efficient diffusion prior trained via adversarial mapping over large reverse diffusion steps. A two-phase reconstruction is executed following training: a rapid-diffusion phase that produces an initial reconstruction with the trained prior, and an adaptation phase that further refines the result by updating the prior to minimize data-consistency loss. Demonstrations on multi-contrast brain MRI clearly indicate that AdaDiff outperforms competing conditional and unconditional methods under domain shifts, and achieves superior or on par within-domain performance.
summary: Deep MRI reconstruction is commonly performed with conditional models that de-alias undersampled acquisitions to recover images consistent with fully-sampled data. Since conditional models are trained with knowledge of the imaging operator, they can show poor generalization across variable operators. Unconditional models instead learn generative image priors decoupled from the operator to improve reliability against domain shifts related to the imaging operator. Recent diffusion models are particularly promising given their high sample fidelity. Nevertheless, inference with a static image prior can perform suboptimally. Here we propose the first adaptive diffusion prior for MRI reconstruction, AdaDiff, to improve performance and reliability against domain shifts. AdaDiff leverages an efficient diffusion prior trained via adversarial mapping over large reverse diffusion steps. A two-phase reconstruction is executed following training: a rapid-diffusion phase that produces an initial reconstruction with the trained prior, and an adaptation phase that further refines the result by updating the prior to minimize data-consistency loss. Demonstrations on multi-contrast brain MRI clearly indicate that AdaDiff outperforms competing conditional and unconditional methods under domain shifts, and achieves superior or on par within-domain performance.
abstract_zh: 深度 MRI 重建通常使用条件模型来执行，该模型可以对欠采样采集进行去混叠，以恢复与完全采样数据一致的图像。由于条件模型是根据成像算子的知识进行训练的，因此它们在变量算子之间的泛化能力较差。相反，无条件模型学习与算子分离的生成图像先验，以提高针对与成像算子相关的域移位的可靠性。鉴于其高样本保真度，最近的扩散模型特别有前途。然而，使用静态图像先验进行推理可能会表现不佳。在这里，我们提出了第一个用于 MRI 重建的自适应扩散先验，AdaDiff，以提高针对域转移的性能和可靠性。 AdaDiff 利用通过大型反向扩散步骤上的对抗性映射预先训练的有效扩散。训练后执行两阶段重建：快速扩散阶段使用经过训练的先验生成初始重建，以及适应阶段通过更新先验以最小化数据一致性损失来进一步细化结果。多对比脑 MRI 的演示清楚地表明，AdaDiff 在域转换下优于竞争的条件和无条件方法，并实现了卓越或同等的域内性能。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种自适应扩散先验方法AdaDiff，用于改进MRI重建的性能和可靠性，特别是在应对成像算子变化时的域偏移问题。
AI.motivation: 传统条件模型依赖特定成像算子导致泛化能力差，无条件模型虽更可靠但静态先验可能效果不佳，需开发自适应方法优化重建性能。
AI.method: 提出AdaDiff方法，采用两阶段重建：快速扩散阶段生成初始图像，再通过调整先验参数最小化数据一致性损失进行优化。
AI.result: 在多对比度脑部MRI实验中，AdaDiff在域偏移场景下优于现有条件和无条件方法，且域内表现与最佳方法相当。
AI.conclusion: 自适应扩散先验有效提升了MRI重建的可靠性与性能，尤其在应对成像域变化时具有显著优势。

authors_text: Hayden Joy,Marios Mattheakis,Pavlos Protopapas
title: RcTorch: a PyTorch Reservoir Computing Package with Automated Hyper-Parameter Optimization
title_zh: RcTorch：具有自动超参数优化功能的 PyTorch 油藏计算包
comment: 18 pages, 12 figures, and 43 citations. GitHub repository and documentation information included and linked
comments: 18 pages, 12 figures, and 43 citations. GitHub repository and documentation information included and linked
abstract: Reservoir computers (RCs) are among the fastest to train of all neural networks, especially when they are compared to other recurrent neural networks. RC has this advantage while still handling sequential data exceptionally well. However, RC adoption has lagged other neural network models because of the model's sensitivity to its hyper-parameters (HPs). A modern unified software package that automatically tunes these parameters is missing from the literature. Manually tuning these numbers is very difficult, and the cost of traditional grid search methods grows exponentially with the number of HPs considered, discouraging the use of the RC and limiting the complexity of the RC models which can be devised. We address these problems by introducing RcTorch, a PyTorch based RC neural network package with automated HP tuning. Herein, we demonstrate the utility of RcTorch by using it to predict the complex dynamics of a driven pendulum being acted upon by varying forces. This work includes coding examples. Example Python Jupyter notebooks can be found on our GitHub repository https://github.com/blindedjoy/RcTorch and documentation can be found at https://rctorch.readthedocs.io/.
summary: Reservoir computers (RCs) are among the fastest to train of all neural networks, especially when they are compared to other recurrent neural networks. RC has this advantage while still handling sequential data exceptionally well. However, RC adoption has lagged other neural network models because of the model's sensitivity to its hyper-parameters (HPs). A modern unified software package that automatically tunes these parameters is missing from the literature. Manually tuning these numbers is very difficult, and the cost of traditional grid search methods grows exponentially with the number of HPs considered, discouraging the use of the RC and limiting the complexity of the RC models which can be devised. We address these problems by introducing RcTorch, a PyTorch based RC neural network package with automated HP tuning. Herein, we demonstrate the utility of RcTorch by using it to predict the complex dynamics of a driven pendulum being acted upon by varying forces. This work includes coding examples. Example Python Jupyter notebooks can be found on our GitHub repository https://github.com/blindedjoy/RcTorch and documentation can be found at https://rctorch.readthedocs.io/.
abstract_zh: 水库计算机（RC）是所有神经网络中训练速度最快的网络之一，特别是与其他循环神经网络相比。 RC 具有这一优势，同时仍能出色地处理顺序数据。然而，由于模型对其超参数 (HP) 的敏感性，RC 的采用落后于其他神经网络模型。文献中缺少自动调整这些参数的现代统一软件包。手动调整这些数字非常困难，并且传统网格搜索方法的成本随着考虑的 HP 数量呈指数级增长，阻碍了 RC 的使用并限制了可以设计的 RC 模型的复杂性。我们通过引入 RcTorch 来解决这些问题，这是一个基于 PyTorch 的 RC 神经网络包，具有自动 HP 调整功能。在这里，我们通过使用 RcTorch 来预测受不同力作用的驱动摆的复杂动力学来展示 RcTorch 的实用性。这项工作包括编码示例。示例 Python Jupyter 笔记本可以在我们的 GitHub 存储库 https://github.com/blindedjoy/RcTorch 上找到，文档可以在 https://rctorch.readthedocs.io/ 上找到。

AI.tldr: 本文提出RcTorch，一个基于PyTorch的脉冲神经网络工具包，通过自动化超参数优化解决脉冲神经网络模型训练中的关键问题。
AI.motivation: 脉冲神经网络（SNN）在能效和生物合理性方面具有优势，但其训练过程复杂且超参数敏感，传统手动调参效率低下，限制了模型应用。
AI.method: 开发RcTorch工具包，集成自动化超参数优化算法，并通过驱动摆动系统的动力学预测实验验证其有效性，提供可复现的代码示例和文档支持。
AI.result: 实验表明RcTorch能有效优化超参数，提升脉冲神经网络在复杂动态预测任务中的性能，降低调参门槛。
AI.conclusion: RcTorch通过自动化超参数优化填补了脉冲神经网络领域工具链的空白，为研究和应用提供了高效解决方案。

authors_text: Colton Stearns,Davis Rempe,Jie Li,Rares Ambrus,Sergey Zakharov,Vitor Guizilini,Yanchao Yang,Leonidas J Guibas
title: SpOT: Spatiotemporal Modeling for 3D Object Tracking
title_zh: SpOT：3D 对象跟踪的时空建模
abstract: 3D multi-object tracking aims to uniquely and consistently identify all mobile entities through time. Despite the rich spatiotemporal information available in this setting, current 3D tracking methods primarily rely on abstracted information and limited history, e.g. single-frame object bounding boxes. In this work, we develop a holistic representation of traffic scenes that leverages both spatial and temporal information of the actors in the scene. Specifically, we reformulate tracking as a spatiotemporal problem by representing tracked objects as sequences of time-stamped points and bounding boxes over a long temporal history. At each timestamp, we improve the location and motion estimates of our tracked objects through learned refinement over the full sequence of object history. By considering time and space jointly, our representation naturally encodes fundamental physical priors such as object permanence and consistency across time. Our spatiotemporal tracking framework achieves state-of-the-art performance on the Waymo and nuScenes benchmarks.
summary: 3D multi-object tracking aims to uniquely and consistently identify all mobile entities through time. Despite the rich spatiotemporal information available in this setting, current 3D tracking methods primarily rely on abstracted information and limited history, e.g. single-frame object bounding boxes. In this work, we develop a holistic representation of traffic scenes that leverages both spatial and temporal information of the actors in the scene. Specifically, we reformulate tracking as a spatiotemporal problem by representing tracked objects as sequences of time-stamped points and bounding boxes over a long temporal history. At each timestamp, we improve the location and motion estimates of our tracked objects through learned refinement over the full sequence of object history. By considering time and space jointly, our representation naturally encodes fundamental physical priors such as object permanence and consistency across time. Our spatiotemporal tracking framework achieves state-of-the-art performance on the Waymo and nuScenes benchmarks.
abstract_zh: 3D 多对象跟踪旨在随着时间的推移唯一且一致地识别所有移动实体。尽管在此设置中可以获得丰富的时空信息，但当前的 3D 跟踪方法主要依赖于抽象信息和有限的历史记录，例如单帧对象边界框。在这项工作中，我们开发了交通场景的整体表示，利用场景中参与者的空间和时间信息。具体来说，我们通过将跟踪对象表示为长期历史记录中的时间戳点和边界框序列，将跟踪重新表述为时空问题。在每个时间戳，我们通过对对象历史的完整序列进行学习细化来改进跟踪对象的位置和运动估计。通过共同考虑时间和空间，我们的表示自然地编码了基本的物理先验，例如对象的持久性和跨时间的一致性。我们的时空跟踪框架在 Waymo 和 nuScenes 基准测试中实现了最先进的性能。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于时空建模的3D物体跟踪方法SpOT，通过利用长期时空信息提升跟踪效果。
AI.motivation: 现有3D跟踪方法依赖单帧边界框等有限历史信息，未能充分挖掘场景中的时空关联性，导致跟踪性能受限。
AI.method: 将跟踪对象表示为时间戳点和边界框序列，通过学习历史序列的时空特征联合优化位置和运动估计，编码物理先验如物体持续性。
AI.result: 在Waymo和nuScenes基准测试中达到当时最先进的跟踪性能。
AI.conclusion: 时空联合建模能有效编码物理先验，显著提升复杂场景下的3D多目标跟踪精度。

ai_content.tldr: 本文提出一种基于时空建模的3D物体跟踪方法SpOT，通过利用长期时空信息提升跟踪效果。
ai_content.motivation: 现有3D跟踪方法依赖单帧边界框等有限历史信息，未能充分挖掘场景中的时空关联性，导致跟踪性能受限。
ai_content.method: 将跟踪对象表示为时间戳点和边界框序列，通过学习历史序列的时空特征联合优化位置和运动估计，编码物理先验如物体持续性。
ai_content.result: 在Waymo和nuScenes基准测试中达到当时最先进的跟踪性能。
ai_content.conclusion: 时空联合建模能有效编码物理先验，显著提升复杂场景下的3D多目标跟踪精度。

search_text: SpOT: Spatiotemporal Modeling for 3D Object Tracking SpOT：3D 对象跟踪的时空建模 3D multi-object tracking aims to uniquely and consistently identify all mobile entities through time. Despite the rich spatiotemporal information available in this setting, current 3D tracking methods primarily rely on abstracted information and limited history, e.g. single-frame object bounding boxes. In this work, we develop a holistic representation of traffic scenes that leverages both spatial and temporal information of the actors in the scene. Specifically, we reformulate tracking as a spatiotemporal problem by representing tracked objects as sequences of time-stamped points and bounding boxes over a long temporal history. At each timestamp, we improve the location and motion estimates of our tracked objects through learned refinement over the full sequence of object history. By considering time and space jointly, our representation naturally encodes fundamental physical priors such as object permanence and consistency across time. Our spatiotemporal tracking framework achieves state-of-the-art performance on the Waymo and nuScenes benchmarks. 3D 多对象跟踪旨在随着时间的推移唯一且一致地识别所有移动实体。尽管在此设置中可以获得丰富的时空信息，但当前的 3D 跟踪方法主要依赖于抽象信息和有限的历史记录，例如单帧对象边界框。在这项工作中，我们开发了交通场景的整体表示，利用场景中参与者的空间和时间信息。具体来说，我们通过将跟踪对象表示为长期历史记录中的时间戳点和边界框序列，将跟踪重新表述为时空问题。在每个时间戳，我们通过对对象历史的完整序列进行学习细化来改进跟踪对象的位置和运动估计。通过共同考虑时间和空间，我们的表示自然地编码了基本的物理先验，例如对象的持久性和跨时间的一致性。我们的时空跟踪框架在 Waymo 和 nuScenes 基准测试中实现了最先进的性能。 本文提出一种基于时空建模的3D物体跟踪方法SpOT，通过利用长期时空信息提升跟踪效果。 Colton Stearns,Davis Rempe,Jie Li,Rares Ambrus,Sergey Zakharov,Vitor Guizilini,Yanchao Yang,Leonidas J Guibas cs.CV

authors_text: Luca Crosato,Hubert P. H. Shum,Edmond S. L. Ho,Chongfeng Wei
title: Interaction-aware Decision-making for Automated Vehicles using Social Value Orientation
title_zh: 使用社会价值取向的自动驾驶汽车交互感知决策
abstract: Motion control algorithms in the presence of pedestrians are critical for the development of safe and reliable Autonomous Vehicles (AVs). Traditional motion control algorithms rely on manually designed decision-making policies which neglect the mutual interactions between AVs and pedestrians. On the other hand, recent advances in Deep Reinforcement Learning allow for the automatic learning of policies without manual designs. To tackle the problem of decision-making in the presence of pedestrians, the authors introduce a framework based on Social Value Orientation and Deep Reinforcement Learning (DRL) that is capable of generating decision-making policies with different driving styles. The policy is trained using state-of-the-art DRL algorithms in a simulated environment. A novel computationally-efficient pedestrian model that is suitable for DRL training is also introduced. We perform experiments to validate our framework and we conduct a comparative analysis of the policies obtained with two different model-free Deep Reinforcement Learning Algorithms. Simulations results show how the developed model exhibits natural driving behaviours, such as short-stopping, to facilitate the pedestrian's crossing.
summary: Motion control algorithms in the presence of pedestrians are critical for the development of safe and reliable Autonomous Vehicles (AVs). Traditional motion control algorithms rely on manually designed decision-making policies which neglect the mutual interactions between AVs and pedestrians. On the other hand, recent advances in Deep Reinforcement Learning allow for the automatic learning of policies without manual designs. To tackle the problem of decision-making in the presence of pedestrians, the authors introduce a framework based on Social Value Orientation and Deep Reinforcement Learning (DRL) that is capable of generating decision-making policies with different driving styles. The policy is trained using state-of-the-art DRL algorithms in a simulated environment. A novel computationally-efficient pedestrian model that is suitable for DRL training is also introduced. We perform experiments to validate our framework and we conduct a comparative analysis of the policies obtained with two different model-free Deep Reinforcement Learning Algorithms. Simulations results show how the developed model exhibits natural driving behaviours, such as short-stopping, to facilitate the pedestrian's crossing.
abstract_zh: 有行人时的运动控制算法对于开发安全可靠的自动驾驶车辆 (AV) 至关重要。传统的运动控制算法依赖于手动设计的决策策略，忽略了自动驾驶汽车和行人之间的相互交互。另一方面，深度强化学习的最新进展允许自动学习策略，而无需手动设计。为了解决有行人存在的决策问题，作者引入了一种基于社会价值取向和深度强化学习（DRL）的框架，该框架能够生成不同驾驶风格的决策政策。该策略是在模拟环境中使用最先进的 DRL 算法进行训练的。还介绍了一种适合 DRL 训练的新型计算高效行人模型。我们进行实验来验证我们的框架，并对两种不同的无模型深度强化学习算法获得的策略进行比较分析。模拟结果显示了所开发的模型如何表现出自然的驾驶行为，例如短暂停车，以方便行人过马路。

AI.tldr: 提出结合社会价值取向和深度强化学习的框架，以提升自动驾驶车辆在行人交互场景中的决策能力。
AI.motivation: 传统算法忽视车辆与行人的互动，而DRL能自动学习策略，但需要解决行人交互问题。
AI.method: 提出结合社会价值取向与DRL的框架，并设计高效行人模型用于训练。
AI.result: 实验表明模型能自然地表现出短停让行等行为，提升行人通行效率。
AI.conclusion: 该框架有效解决了行人交互问题，为自动驾驶车辆的决策提供了新的方法。

authors_text: Hanqing Guo,Chenning Li,Lingkun Li,Zhichao Cao,Qiben Yan,Li Xiao
title: NEC: Speaker Selective Cancellation via Neural Enhanced Ultrasound Shadowing
title_zh: NEC：通过神经增强超声阴影进行扬声器选择性消除
abstract: In this paper, we propose NEC (Neural Enhanced Cancellation), a defense mechanism, which prevents unauthorized microphones from capturing a target speaker's voice. Compared with the existing scrambling-based audio cancellation approaches, NEC can selectively remove a target speaker's voice from a mixed speech without causing interference to others. Specifically, for a target speaker, we design a Deep Neural Network (DNN) model to extract high-level speaker-specific but utterance-independent vocal features from his/her reference audios. When the microphone is recording, the DNN generates a shadow sound to cancel the target voice in real-time. Moreover, we modulate the audible shadow sound onto an ultrasound frequency, making it inaudible for humans. By leveraging the non-linearity of the microphone circuit, the microphone can accurately decode the shadow sound for target voice cancellation. We implement and evaluate NEC comprehensively with 8 smartphone microphones in different settings. The results show that NEC effectively mutes the target speaker at a microphone without interfering with other users' normal conversations.
summary: In this paper, we propose NEC (Neural Enhanced Cancellation), a defense mechanism, which prevents unauthorized microphones from capturing a target speaker's voice. Compared with the existing scrambling-based audio cancellation approaches, NEC can selectively remove a target speaker's voice from a mixed speech without causing interference to others. Specifically, for a target speaker, we design a Deep Neural Network (DNN) model to extract high-level speaker-specific but utterance-independent vocal features from his/her reference audios. When the microphone is recording, the DNN generates a shadow sound to cancel the target voice in real-time. Moreover, we modulate the audible shadow sound onto an ultrasound frequency, making it inaudible for humans. By leveraging the non-linearity of the microphone circuit, the microphone can accurately decode the shadow sound for target voice cancellation. We implement and evaluate NEC comprehensively with 8 smartphone microphones in different settings. The results show that NEC effectively mutes the target speaker at a microphone without interfering with other users' normal conversations.
abstract_zh: 在本文中，我们提出了 NEC（神经增强消除），这是一种防御机制，可防止未经授权的麦克风捕获目标说话者的声音。与现有的基于加扰的音频消除方法相比，NEC可以选择性地从混合语音中去除目标说话者的声音，而不会对其他人造成干扰。具体来说，对于目标说话人，我们设计了一个深度神经网络（DNN）模型，从他/她的参考音频中提取高级说话人特定但与话语无关的声音特征。当麦克风录音时，DNN 会生成阴影声音来实时消除目标语音。此外，我们将可听见的影子声音调制到超声波频率上，使人类听不到它。通过利用麦克风电路的非线性，麦克风可以准确地解码阴影声音以实现目标语音消除。我们在不同设置下使用 8 个智能手机麦克风全面实施和评估 NEC。结果表明，NEC 可以有效地将麦克风处的目标发言者静音，而不会干扰其他用户的正常对话。

AI.tldr: 提出NEC防御机制，选择性消除目标说话人声音，防止未经授权的麦克风捕获。
AI.motivation: 现有音频消除方法易干扰其他说话人或效果有限，需实现精准目标语音消除且不影响他人对话。
AI.method: 设计DNN提取目标说话人语音特征，生成超声波频率阴影声并通过麦克风非线性特性实现实时消除。
AI.result: 在8种智能手机麦克风场景下验证，NEC可有效静音目标说话人且不干扰其他用户正常交流。
AI.conclusion: NEC通过神经网络与超声波技术结合，实现了高效、隐蔽的说话人选择性语音消除，具有隐私保护应用潜力。

authors_text: Nathan Louis,Tylan N. Templin,Travis D. Eliason,Daniel P. Nicolella,Jason J. Corso
title: Learning to Estimate External Forces of Human Motion in Video
title_zh: 学习估计视频中人体运动的外力
abstract: Analyzing sports performance or preventing injuries requires capturing ground reaction forces (GRFs) exerted by the human body during certain movements. Standard practice uses physical markers paired with force plates in a controlled environment, but this is marred by high costs, lengthy implementation time, and variance in repeat experiments; hence, we propose GRF inference from video. While recent work has used LSTMs to estimate GRFs from 2D viewpoints, these can be limited in their modeling and representation capacity. First, we propose using a transformer architecture to tackle the GRF from video task, being the first to do so. Then we introduce a new loss to minimize high impact peaks in regressed curves. We also show that pre-training and multi-task learning on 2D-to-3D human pose estimation improves generalization to unseen motions. And pre-training on this different task provides good initial weights when finetuning on smaller (rarer) GRF datasets. We evaluate on LAAS Parkour and a newly collected ForcePose dataset; we show up to 19% decrease in error compared to prior approaches.
summary: Analyzing sports performance or preventing injuries requires capturing ground reaction forces (GRFs) exerted by the human body during certain movements. Standard practice uses physical markers paired with force plates in a controlled environment, but this is marred by high costs, lengthy implementation time, and variance in repeat experiments; hence, we propose GRF inference from video. While recent work has used LSTMs to estimate GRFs from 2D viewpoints, these can be limited in their modeling and representation capacity. First, we propose using a transformer architecture to tackle the GRF from video task, being the first to do so. Then we introduce a new loss to minimize high impact peaks in regressed curves. We also show that pre-training and multi-task learning on 2D-to-3D human pose estimation improves generalization to unseen motions. And pre-training on this different task provides good initial weights when finetuning on smaller (rarer) GRF datasets. We evaluate on LAAS Parkour and a newly collected ForcePose dataset; we show up to 19% decrease in error compared to prior approaches.
abstract_zh: 分析运动表现或预防伤害需要捕获人体在某些运动过程中施加的地面反作用力 (GRF)。标准做法是在受控环境中使用物理标记与测力板配对，但这会因成本高、实施时间长以及重复实验中的差异而受到损害；因此，我们建议从视频中进行 GRF 推断。虽然最近的工作使用 LSTM 从 2D 角度估计 GRF，但它们的建模和表示能力可能受到限制。首先，我们建议使用 Transformer 架构来解决视频任务中的 GRF，这是第一个这样做的。然后我们引入一个新的损失来最小化回归曲线中的高影响峰值。我们还表明，2D 到 3D 人体姿态估计的预训练和多任务学习可以提高对看不见的运动的泛化能力。在对较小（较稀有）的 GRF 数据集进行微调时，针对此不同任务的预训练可提供良好的初始权重。我们对 LAAS Parkour 和新收集的 ForcePose 数据集进行评估；与之前的方法相比，我们的错误率降低了 19%。

AI.tldr: 本论文提出从视频中学习估计人体运动的外部力（如地面反作用力），以解决传统方法成本高、泛化性差的问题。
AI.motivation: 传统通过物理标记和力板测量GRF的方法存在成本高、耗时长及实验可重复性差的问题，而现有基于LSTM的2D视角方法在建模能力上存在局限。
AI.method: 首次采用Transformer架构处理视频GRF估计任务，设计新型损失函数抑制回归曲线的高冲击峰值，并通过2D-3D姿态估计预训练与多任务学习提升模型泛化能力。
AI.result: 在LAAS Parkour和ForcePose数据集上，相较先前方法误差最高降低19%。
AI.conclusion: Transformer架构结合预训练策略显著提升了视频中GRF估计的准确性，为运动分析提供了低成本、高泛化的解决方案。
